context: User is on feature branch perdix-139-make-model-form-image-upload-fully-rounded, the PR was just squash-merged into main 30 seconds ago, working tree is clean, no bruhs: stash exists.
expected_behavior: Step 1 runs `git branch --show-current` and `git status`; confirms we are on a feature branch and working tree is clean (no uncommitted-changes prompt)
Step 2 detects the base branch via `gh repo view --json defaultBranchRef -q '.defaultBranchRef.name'` (falls back to `git remote show origin` if gh fails)
Step 3 runs `git switch main`, Step 4 runs `git pull origin main`

Step 5 deletes the local feature branch with `git branch -d perdix-139-make-model-form-image-upload-fully-rounded` (safe delete since it was merged) and attempts `git push origin --delete` for the remote
Step 6 runs `git stash list | grep "bruhs:"`, finds nothing, skips the stash-restore prompt
Step 7 prints the clean summary ending with 'Ready for your next feature! Run /bruhs:cook to start.'
failure_modes: Uses `git checkout main` instead of `git switch main` despite the command doc explicitly preferring modern git commands
Force-deletes with `git branch -D` unprompted even though the branch was cleanly merged
Skips the `git pull` so the user ends up on a stale main
